evidence_snippets: 98% during the critical window
get it back within about an hour
Aim for 98% of user workstations
Min hardware: ~400MB free disk
coverage_entries.reason: Notes require 98% compliance and 400MB free disk.

evidence_snippets: Software: IE browser, .NET Framework 1.1, IE Web Controls
We’ll try to get 98% compliance before go-live
We will deliver seven files
one business day before the budgeting kickoff
plan for a 3-hour processing window
coverage_entries.reason: BDW will send 7 files, Oct snapshot, 1-day lead, 3-hour load.

evidence_snippets: within 1 week of Finance delivering changes
then 2 weeks of joint testing
coverage_entries.reason: Source mandates 1 week implementation then 2 weeks joint testing.

evidence_snippets: one hundred percent — must be in RFS
includes all organizational units
coverage_entries.reason: Stakeholders require 100% of October snapshot orgs/accounts in RFS.

evidence_snippets: integrate with UAS for identities. We won’t maintain local accounts
UAS will be the sole source for authentication and authorization
coverage_entries.reason: All access/authentication must come from UAS; no local accounts.

evidence_snippets: Functionality will be limited by role. The UI will hide what you don’t have permission for
Map to PCG hierarchy levels (branch, complex, district, region)
coverage_entries.reason: Access limited by role; roles map to PCG hierarchy levels.

evidence_snippets: Historically about 95% is approved pre-release
small tail we fix within a month
coverage_entries.reason: ENET architecture pre-approval target ~95%, remainder fixed within month.

evidence_snippets: support from 800x600 and higher
aim to have most of them correct before rollout
we will correct it within a week
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require 800x600+ support, most correct pre-rollout, fixes within week.

evidence_snippets: one week of training
98% of users finish budgets
coverage_entries.reason: One-week training and 98% completion target stated.

evidence_snippets: 2 sec response for 90% interactions
up to 10 sec acceptable for the rest

unsupported_implied.text: The system and QA process shall collect and log performance metrics (performance statistics) to verify the stated response-time targets (e.g., record stats to show 90% of interactions <= 2s and 10% <= 10s).
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: Response times — log performance stats in QA to ensure 90% under 2 sec, 10% under 10 sec.
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold set specifies response-time targets but does not require the system/QA to collect or log performance metrics. Instrumentation and logging to demonstrate compliance is a distinct operational capability implied by the source.

unsupported_implied.text: The RFS shall not implement any new in‑app notification features for events such as BDW load failures or access changes; communications must use existing helpdesk/ops channels instead.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: we're not putting any new notification features inside RFS.
unsupported_implied.reason: The source explicitly rules out adding in-app notification features and mandates relying on existing helpdesk/operations communication channels. The gold requirements do not state this prohibition or communication constraint, so this is an additional functional limitation on the product.
